title: Model registry stages and their meaning
description: How None/Staging/Production/Archived stages and version aliases structure controlled model rollout.
body: Use when reasoning about how a registry controls which model serves. Registries model a version's lifecycle as stages — none, staging, production, archived — plus aliases that point named slots at versions. Do not point a serving alias straight at a freshly logged version; route it through staging first. Actions: register a version, transition it to staging for gating, then move the production alias on a passing gate, and archive superseded versions. Validation signals include an alias whose target resolves to a gated version and an audit log of stage transitions. Risk boundary: a serving alias jumping to an ungated version skips all guardrails; require a recorded gate pass before the production transition or escalate.
